title: Wild and Wacky World of Wigs
titleZh: 疯狂又古怪的假发世界

q.en: According to the book where and when were the oldest-known wigs found?
q.zh: 根据本书，已知最古老的假发是在哪里、何时出现的？

options.en: In China around 2000 BC. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 在中国，约公元前2000年。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book states the oldest-known wigs date back to about 1400 BC in Egypt.
explain.zh: 书中说，已知最古老的假发可追溯到约公元前1400年的埃及。

q.en: Why did ancient Egyptians wear wigs?
q.zh: 古埃及人为什么戴假发？

options.en: Only to keep warm in winter.
Because they had no hair at all. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 因为他们根本不长头发。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To look like their gods and to cover bald spots.
options.zh: 为了看起来像他们的神，并遮掩秃顶。

explain.en: Egyptians wore wigs to resemble their gods and to hide bald spots since a full head of hair meant health and strength.
explain.zh: 埃及人戴假发是为了像神一样，并遮掩秃顶，因为浓密的头发象征健康与力量。

q.en: Who brought wigs back into fashion in France in the early 17th century?
q.zh: 17世纪初，是谁让假发在法国重新流行起来的？

options.en: Julius Caesar. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 尤利乌斯·凯撒。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: King Louis XIII began losing his hair at 23 and wore wigs and noblemen copied him to appear fashionable.
explain.zh: 路易十三在23岁开始脱发并戴假发，贵族们纷纷效仿以显时髦。

q.en: After other remedies failed what did Julius Caesar use to cover his baldness?
q.zh: 在其他方法都失败后，尤利乌斯·凯撒用什么来遮掩自己的秃顶？

explain.en: Caesar wore a laurel wreath which also symbolized victory to cover his bald spot.
explain.zh: 凯撒戴上月桂冠来遮秃顶，而月桂冠也象征胜利。

q.en: The book explains wig types materials and history and includes a glossary and photo captions. This organization shows it is...
q.zh: 本书讲解了假发的种类、材质和历史，并配有词汇表和图片说明。这种结构说明它是一篇……

options.en: a fictional story. according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 虚构故事。（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: an informational text organized by topic with supporting features.
options.zh: 按主题组织、配有辅助素材的说明性文章。

explain.en: The clear topic sections glossary and captions are typical of informational writing.
explain.zh: 清晰的主题小节、词汇表和图片说明，都是说明性文本的典型特征。

q.en: The word bigwig meaning an important person came from the fact that...
q.zh: 「bigwig（大人物）」一词意指重要人物，它来源于……

options.en: it is a French word for hat.
options.zh: 它是法语中「帽子」的意思。

options.en: the richest and most influential people wore the biggest fanciest wigs.
options.zh: 最富有、最有影响力的人戴最大最华丽的假发。

options.en: only very tall people wore wigs. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只有很高的人才戴假发。（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: wigs were illegal at the time.

explain.en: Because the most powerful people wore the largest wigs bigwig came to mean an important person.
explain.zh: 由于最有权力的人戴最大的假发，bigwig 便引申为重要人物。

q.en: Using the book's comparison of materials which kind of wig looked the most natural but was the most expensive?
q.zh: 根据书中对材质的比较，哪种假发看起来最自然，却又最昂贵？

options.en: Plastic. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 塑料。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Wigs made from human hair looked most natural but took 6-10 heads of hair each and only the wealthy could afford them.
explain.zh: 人发假发最自然，但每顶需用6到10个人的头发，只有富人买得起。

q.en: The book mentions the organization Locks of Love. What does it do?
q.zh: 书中提到了「Locks of Love」这个组织。它是做什么的？

options.zh: 它为了盈利而出售假发。

options.en: It makes wigs for children who have lost their hair from illness.
options.zh: 它为因疾病脱发的小朋友制作假发。

options.en: It makes costumes for movies. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它为电影制作戏服。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Locks of Love uses donated hair to make wigs for children who have lost their hair.
explain.zh: Locks of Love用捐赠的头发为脱发儿童制作假发。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in Wild and Wacky World of Wigs?
q.zh: 作者写《疯狂又古怪的假发世界》的主要写作目的是什么？

options.en: To inform and entertain readers about the history and uses of wigs.
options.zh: 为了向读者介绍并以趣味方式呈现假发的历史与用途。

options.en: To persuade everyone to wear wigs. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说服每个人都戴假发。（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book blends facts with amusing details to teach and entertain not to persuade or preach.
explain.zh: 本书将事实与有趣细节结合，旨在教学与娱乐，而非说服或说教。

q.en: The title Wild and Wacky World of Wigs and details like rats nesting in hair suggest the author's tone is...
q.zh: 「疯狂又古怪的假发世界」这个标题，加上老鼠在头发里筑巢等细节，说明作者的语气是……

options.en: serious and sorrowful. according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 严肃而悲伤的。（按书中另一种说法）
轻松、幽默而又具知识性的。

explain.en: The playful title and silly facts show the author is having fun while sharing information.
explain.zh: 俏皮的标题和滑稽的事实表明，作者在分享知识的同时也乐在其中。

prompt.en: Wigs have been used for fashion status and hiding baldness for thousands of years. Why do you think people care so much about their hair?
prompt.zh: 数千年来，假发一直被用于时尚、身份地位和遮掩秃顶。你认为人们为何如此在意自己的头发？

en: List two reasons people wore wigs in the book.
Explain how wigs showed social status.
Connect this to today's ideas of appearance and self-esteem.
zh: 列出书中人们戴假发的两个原因。
解释假发如何体现社会地位。
将此与当今对外貌和自尊的看法联系起来。
